arguments.task: Open the official WhatsApp Business developer documentation and read canonical first-party pages that support: product description, authentication methods, API surface, onboarding/access requirements, pricing or plan/access tier, and rate limits. Prefer pages within developers.facebook.com and related official Meta docs. Return the exact URLs visited and concise extracted findings only from page content actually read. If useful, open linked official pages for Cloud API, Embedded Signup, API Setup, Pricing, Rate Limits, and Postman collection/API reference. Avoid third-party sites and search snippets.

raw_result.description: Meta’s WhatsApp Business Platform lets businesses send and receive WhatsApp messages and manage business assets such as accounts and phone numbers via APIs.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official Meta docs for the WhatsApp Business Platform include an Access Tokens page and platform pages that describe app-scoped/user/business system access tokens for API calls, plus embedded signup/business login flows based on Meta app authorization. Evidence gathered supports token-based access tokens and Meta OAuth-style app authorization flows; no first-party evidence was gathered here for API keys or HTTP Basic.
raw_result.gating_reason: Official developer docs present the WhatsApp Business Platform as publicly available developer documentation with setup/onboarding flows in Meta’s self-serve developer environment; no evidence gathered indicated partner-only or contact-sales gating for initial API access.

api_surface.breadth: Official docs describe HTTP/JSON APIs for the WhatsApp Business Platform, including WhatsApp Cloud API and related Business Management API resources for business portfolios, WhatsApp Business Accounts, phone numbers, templates, throughput, and rate-limit-related platform operations.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No first-party evidence was gathered from the official pages read indicating an official MCP server for WhatsApp Business.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: The About the Platform documentation references throughput, scaling, and rate limits as platform considerations, but no specific numeric limits were gathered in the evidence already collected.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official Meta docs explicitly frame the product as HTTP/JSON-based, document business assets and Cloud API concepts, and include a dedicated access-tokens page, which are strong signals of a buildable public developer surface.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Some structured fields remain only partially supported by the gathered page evidence: the exact access-tier classification and the presence of OAuth2 as a documented auth method were inferred from Meta authorization/onboarding context rather than directly confirmed from a specific official auth page captured in this pass; MCP absence is also based on lack of evidence rather than an explicit first-party statement.
